steps.title: Use the graph intercepts
steps.content: The graph shows a decreasing linear relationship with intercepts near $(80,0)$ and $(0,45)$. A correct equation should match both intercepts and should not have a positive slope.

steps.title: Test the vertical intercept
steps.content: When $x=0$, the candidate equation becomes $$9(0)+16y=720$$$$y=45$$So its vertical intercept is the graph's shown value.

steps.title: Test the horizontal intercept
steps.content: For the other endpoint, use $y=0$:$$9x+16(0)=720$$$$x=80$$This gives the horizontal crossing shown on the graph.

steps.title: Select the matching equation
steps.content: The equation with intercepts $(80,0)$ and $(0,45)$ is $9x+16y=720$, which is the listed equation with the correct decreasing relationship. The answer is B.
